title: Set — named collection of entities of one type
description: The general 'named collection of N things of the same kind' pattern. Generalizes the sample / group / interval registries: a sample set is a set with entity_type='sample'; a sites set is a set with entity_type='variant_site'; a window list is a set with entity_type='window'; etc. One JSON file per set under <registry_root>/sets/<entity_type>/<set_id>.json. The flat catalogue set_registry.tsv is generated from these by lib/registry_index.py. Per SETS_AND_ANALYSES.md §'The set registry'. Members are EITHER inline (small sets), a path to a file (large sets), or computed via derived_from (set algebra over parents). The set algebra (intersect / union / difference / filter / from_inline / from_set) is generic over entity_type — see lib/set_algebra.py.

set_id.description: Stable identifier. Convention: '<what>_<scope_tag>_v<N>' (e.g. 'sites_LG28_localpca_v1', 'samples_broodstock226_v1', 'windows_50kb_LG28_v1', 'candidates_D17_PASS_v1'). For content-hashed sample sets (per sample_set_v1.schema.json) the prefix is 'sset_' + hex hash.

entity_type.description: What KIND of thing is in this set. Canonical values per entity_type_v1.schema.json: sample, variant_site, window, interval, inversion_candidate, breakpoint, gene, te_copy, marker, chromosome, scaffold, contig, bam, … Atlases may add their own.

path.description: Relative or absolute path to the file holding the members. Conventions per entity_type: .tsv / .tsv.gz with a known column schema (id-only, or id + coord, or full row). Optional when derived_from is present and the registry resolves members on demand.

n_entities.description: Number of members. Stored for fast filtering; should match the resolved member count.

hash.description: sha256 of the canonical member representation (sorted ids, '\n'-joined, newline-terminated, UTF-8). Same convention as lib/set_algebra.canonical_members(). Two sets that resolve to the same members share this hash; the inventory and lookup helpers use it for the (groups × analysis × samples × intervals) collapse.

parent_set_id.description: Optional single-parent FK to another set (e.g. 'sites_LG28_localpca_v1' has parent 'snps_maf005_v1'). When the set was derived by a more complex op, use derived_from instead.

derived_from.description: Set algebra lineage. Null/absent for atomic sets (members are inline or read from path). Same shape as sample_set_v1.derived_from but generalized — parents may be any set_id of any entity_type that makes sense for the op.

predicate.description: Only when op='filter'. Tag identifying the filter (e.g. 'maf>0.05', 'distance_to_breakpoint<50kb'). The atlas materializer applies it.

filter_profile_id.description: Optional FK into a filter-profile registry (future) — captures e.g. 'broodstock_filter_v1', 'maf005_thin1k_v1'. Free-form for now.

coordinate_system.description: Genome assembly / coordinate system this set is defined against (e.g. 'fClaHyb_Gar_LG_v1'). Required when entity_type is coordinate_aware; null for sample / bam / chromosome / etc.

intended_use.description: Free-form: 'population genomics', 'local PCA', 'breakpoint analysis', 'atlas review'. Helps the user remember why this set was registered.

_examples._description: Sample set (functionally equivalent to a group_definition with dimension=trivial or to a sample_set_v1 with entity_type=sample).
_examples.label: Broodstock 226 — all sequenced individuals

_examples._description: Sites set used for local PCA on LG28. Coordinate-aware.
_examples.label: LG28 sites — MAF≥0.05, thinned to 1 SNP / kb

_examples._description: Window set — 50 kb tiles across LG28.
_examples.label: LG28 50 kb tiling windows

_examples._description: Inversion candidate set — D17 PASS cohort-level.
_examples.label: D17 PASS inversions (cohort-level)

_examples._description: Gene set derived by filter — genes within 50 kb of any breakpoint.
_examples.label: Genes within 50 kb of an inversion breakpoint
